content.problemStatement:
Once, after a stressful day, Chef decided to relax and visit a casino near his house to gamble. He feels lucky and he's going to bet almost all of his money.

The game Chef is going to play in the casino consists of tossing a die with NNN faces twice. There is a number written on each face of the die (these numbers are not necessarily distinct). In order to win, Chef must get the number AAA on the first toss and the number BBB on the second toss of the die.

The excited viewers want to know the probability that Chef will win the game. Can you help them find that number? Assume that Chef gets each face of the die with the same probability on each toss and that tosses are mutually independent.
constraints: 1‚Č§xi‚Č§N1 \le x_i \le N1‚Č§xi‚Äč‚Č§N for each valid iii
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains three space-separated integers NNN, AAA and BBB.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers x1,x2,‚Ä¶,xNx_1, x_2, \dots, x_Nx1‚Äč,x2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,xN‚Äč denoting the numbers written on the faces of the die.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one real number ‚ÄĒ the probability that Chef will win. Your answer will be considered correct if its absolute error does not exceed 10‚ąí610^{-6}10‚ąí6.
